meta.title: Niwa Ski Area
meta.description: Setana, Hokkaido — free pure rope tow, weekday night skiing 18:00–21:00. Check today's hours at the top of this page.

hero.title:
Niwa
Ski Area
hero.tagline: A Showa-era rope tow you climb by hand — weekday nights from 18:00 only; check today's status at the top of this page.

sanctuary.title:
Pure rope,
still running.
sanctuary.lead: No chairlift — grab the rope and climb under your own power. One of Japan's rare surviving rope tows. Staff will run it even for a solo visitor.
sanctuary.noticeBody: No grooming — natural snow only. Snowboards use a separate short rope course (~150 m). Hours are buried in town PDFs — call before you drive.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Niwa Ski Area on an open snowfield — rope tow

primary.title: Free · ungroomed · private
primary.lead: No crowds and often untracked snow — a micro hill that powder hunters and vloggers call a living fossil of Showa-era skiing.
primary.imgAlt: Ungroomed slope and rope tow at Niwa

secondary.title: Setana's table
secondary.lead: After skiing, Kitahiyama Onsen Hotel or Mitsuwadiner's pork menchi curry — soak and eat in northern Setana.

pass.title:
Free —
weekdays, nights only.
pass.lead: No lift fee. Typical season: early January through late February. Weekdays 18:00–21:00 (night only); weekends/holidays ~10:00–17:00 (variable). Always check the dashboard and call 0137-84-5111 before leaving home.
pass.imgAlt: Night-lit rope tow at Niwa Ski Area

hike.title:
After the rope,
onsen and Setana food.
hike.lead: Sore arms? Day-use bath at Kitahiyama Onsen Hotel. Mitsuwadiner, Shippoya local sake, Kodaten's one-hour "Iwa-shu" cream puffs.
hike.leadCurry: Pair with Taisei Ski Area (same town) for a Japan Sea sunset view loop.

tour.title:
Showa skiing,
Setana rewards.
tour.lead: Afternoon rope tow, evening onsen, local dinner — a one-night or day-trip winter model in Kitahiyama.
